nominal_composition_text: Ni balance, Cr about 22, W about 14, Mo about 2, Co about 5
product_forms: sheet; plate; bar; pipe; combustor fabrications
heat_treatment_summary: Solid-solution-strengthened Ni-Cr-W-Mo alloy; use solution-annealed/high-temperature fabrication logic rather than age-hardening logic.
joining_fabrication_notes: Haynes notes strong fabricability and castability, with excellent long-term thermal stability and oxidation resistance.
operating_temperature_band: Outstanding oxidation resistance up to about 2100F / 1149C is highlighted by Haynes.
application_notes: Combustion cans, transition ducts, flame holders and other gas-turbine hot-section fabrications.
known_watch_outs: Very W-rich chemistry will likely score poorly on sustainability/supply-risk; not an age-hardened gamma-prime analogue.
match_guardrails: Use for W/Mo-containing oxidation-resistant Ni hot-section concepts; do not treat as a 718/282-style age-hardened route.

nominal_composition_text: Ni balance, Mo about 25, Cr about 8
product_forms: bar; plate; rings; casings; duct segments; fasteners
heat_treatment_summary: Age-hardenable Ni-Mo-Cr alloy strengthened by a long-range ordering reaction on aging; use cautious heat-treatment logic, not standard gamma-prime assumptions.
joining_fabrication_notes: Useful for parts requiring lower thermal expansion with good strength and ductility in the aged condition.
operating_temperature_band: Haynes highlights tensile and creep strength properties up to about 1200-1300F / 649-704C.
application_notes: Seal rings, containment rings, duct segments, casings, fasteners and rocket-nozzle style hardware.
known_watch_outs: A specialized Ni-Mo-Cr analogue; not a generic match for Co-rich or Nb/Ti/Al age-hardened superalloys.
match_guardrails: Use when candidate chemistry and application point toward Ni-Mo-Cr stability and thermal-expansion control, not when gamma-prime logic is dominant.

nominal_composition_text: Ni balance, Co about 20, Cr about 20, Mo about 6, Ti about 2.4, Al about 0.5
product_forms: sheet; plate; bar; welded fabrications
heat_treatment_summary: Age-hardenable Ni-Co-Cr-Mo alloy intended to combine good age-hardened strength with strong fabricability and weldability.
joining_fabrication_notes: Haynes emphasizes excellent weldability and ductility in the annealed condition, making 263 useful where fabrication matters.
operating_temperature_band: Good high-temperature strength up to about 1650F / 900C is highlighted in the Haynes brochure.
application_notes: Combustor cans, casings and fabricated gas-turbine structures where weldability matters.
known_watch_outs: Usually not as strong a creep-first analogue as 282 or Waspaloy at the top end; use when fabrication matters materially.
match_guardrails: Prefer for weldable/fabricable Ni-Co-Cr-Mo candidates; do not force-match if the candidate is clearly Nb-rich 718-style or W-rich 230-style.

nominal_composition_text: Ni balance, Cr 20, Co 10, Mo 8.5, Ti 2.1, Al 1.5, Fe max 1.5
product_forms: sheet; plate; rings; closed-die forgings; bar; heavy plate blanks; powder
heat_treatment_summary: Typical solution anneal 2050-2100F; standard age-hardening treatment 1850F / 2 h / AC + 1450F / 8 h / AC. EOS notes as-built parts need solution + aging to develop high-temperature strength.
joining_fabrication_notes: Designed for strong fabricability and weldability relative to creep-capable superalloys; AM feedstock exists for EOS DMLS.
operating_temperature_band: Haynes positions it for high-temperature structural use; EOS positions it for aerospace, rocket engine, turbomachinery and gas-turbine parts.
application_notes: Combustors; turbine and exhaust sections; nozzle components; rocket engine and gas turbine parts.
known_watch_outs: Do not treat as a no-heat-treatment alloy; high-temperature strength depends on proper solution + age route.
match_guardrails: Do not use when the candidate lacks a Ni base or when chemistry is much closer to corrosion-first Ni-Mo systems than precipitation-strengthened superalloys.
provenance_note: Haynes brochure + EOS AM data sheet

nominal_composition_text: Ni 58 min, Cr 20-23, Mo 8-10, Nb+Ta 3.15-4.15, Fe max 5
product_forms: wrought; plate; welded fabrications; corrosion-service components
heat_treatment_summary: Strength comes from Mo and Nb in the Ni-Cr matrix; precipitation-hardening treatments are not required.
joining_fabrication_notes: Excellent fabricability including joining; filler metals based on 625 are used without post-weld heat treatment to maintain strength and ductility.
operating_temperature_band: Service temperatures from cryogenic to about 1800F / 982C are noted in the bulletin.
application_notes: Sea-water and severe-corrosion service; high-strength ductile welded fabrications.
known_watch_outs: Usually a corrosion/service analogue rather than the best creep-first hot-section analogue; avoid over-claiming AM route support from this starter row.
match_guardrails: Do not force-match to gamma-prime-style age-hardened Ni-superalloy candidates where IN718/282-like logic is a better fit.

product_forms: round bar; forging stock; turbine hardware
heat_treatment_summary: Precipitation-hardenable Ni-Fe-Cr turbine alloy; treat as wrought/forged solution-plus-age analogue rather than corrosion-service alloy.
joining_fabrication_notes: Special Metals positions 706 as similar in role to 718 but more readily fabricated, particularly by machining.
operating_temperature_band: Primary uses include aerospace and land-based gas-turbine parts requiring creep and stress-rupture resistance up to about 1300F / 704C.
application_notes: Gas-turbine discs, shafts and other high-strength fabricable turbine components.
known_watch_outs: Lower-temperature hot-section and turbine-disc analogue rather than an ultra-high-temperature combustor sheet alloy.
match_guardrails: Prefer for Ni-Fe-Cr age-hardenable candidates with Nb/Ti support; do not force-match to Co-dominant or refractory chemistries.

spec_ids: UNS N07718; AMS 5662; AMS 5664; W.Nr 2.4668; DIN NiCr19Fe19NbMo3
product_forms: wrought; sheet; rings; casings; fasteners; powder
heat_treatment_summary: Solution treat 1850-1900F, then age at 1450F for 6-8 h and air cool; AM parts are precipitation hardened after build.
joining_fabrication_notes: Good weldability and resistance to post-weld cracking; AM parts can be machined, welded, shot-peened, polished and coated.
operating_temperature_band: Cryogenic service noted; EOS AM sheet highlights good tensile/fatigue/creep/rupture strength up to ~700C / 1290F.
application_notes: Gas turbine components; instrumentation parts; rocket and power-industry parts.
known_watch_outs: Requires precipitation hardening; manage distortion/cracking risk in AM builds and post-build heat treatment.
match_guardrails: Do not use when Co is dominant or when chemistry drifts strongly toward corrosion-first Ni-Mo alloys rather than age-hardened Ni superalloys.
provenance_note: Special Metals bulletin + EOS AM data sheet

product_forms: sheet; plate; bar; forging stock; springs; fasteners
heat_treatment_summary: Precipitation-hardenable Ni-Cr alloy; heat-treated material retains useful strength beyond 1300F and to about 1800F for some applications.
joining_fabrication_notes: Strong general-purpose Ni superalloy analogue for fasteners, springs and gas-turbine hardware; welding requires age-hardenable alloy discipline.
operating_temperature_band: Special Metals highlights high strength to about 1300F / 700C and useful heat-treated strength to about 1800F / 982C.
application_notes: Gas turbine engineering, springs, tooling, fasteners and high-temperature fixtures.
known_watch_outs: Versatile but not the first analogue for AM-first precipitation-hardening routes; use cautiously for crack-sensitive additive concepts.
match_guardrails: Do not prefer over 718/282/Waspaloy when the candidate is clearly a Nb/Mo-rich age-hardened hot-section superalloy.
provenance_note: Special Metals technical bulletin + quick reference guide

nominal_composition_text: Ni balance, Cr about 19, Co about 13.5, Mo about 4.3, Ti about 3.0, Al about 1.4, Fe max about 2
product_forms: wrought; forgings; bars; discs; shafts; fasteners
heat_treatment_summary: Age-hardenable Ni-base superalloy; use wrought/forged solution and age style logic rather than solid-solution-only treatment.
joining_fabrication_notes: Best positioned as a wrought or forged superalloy analogue for rotating hardware; fabrication is more demanding than 282/263-style alloys.
operating_temperature_band: Critical rotating applications to about 1200F / 650C and less-demanding service to about 1600F / 870C are highlighted by Special Metals.
application_notes: Gas-turbine and aerospace rotating components such as shafts, discs, seals, fasteners and springs.
known_watch_outs: Not the easiest weld/fabricate route; avoid over-claiming AM readiness without process-specific evidence.
match_guardrails: Use for creep/strength-led Ni superalloy analogues, not for corrosion-first Ni-Mo systems or lightweight Ti-style prompts.

nominal_composition_text: Ti balance, Mo 14-16, Fe max 0.1, O max 0.15
product_forms: bar; billet; plate; sheet
heat_treatment_summary: ATI lists anneal around 1450-1800F and notes strong beta/alpha+beta processing sensitivity; use beta titanium route logic.
joining_fabrication_notes: Very good hot and cold workability is reported for this beta titanium alloy, but machining and spring-back require attention.
operating_temperature_band: Use as a corrosion-resistant, high-strength beta titanium analogue rather than a very-high-temperature compressor alloy.
application_notes: Specialized structural and corrosion-resistant titanium use cases; often selected for formability and beta-titanium behavior.
known_watch_outs: Mo-rich beta titanium chemistry differs materially from Ti64/Ti6242 route logic; do not over-claim hot-section temperature capability.
match_guardrails: Prefer when candidate chemistry is Ti-Mo beta-leaning; do not force-match to alpha-beta Ti64 family merely because both are titanium.

nominal_composition_text: Ti balance, Al 3, V 8, Cr 6, Mo 4, Zr 4
product_forms: bar; billet; springs; high-strength parts
heat_treatment_summary: Metastable beta titanium alloy; strong solution-treated-and-aged route logic with attention to spring-back and machining behavior.
joining_fabrication_notes: ATI highlights exceptional ductility in the solution-treated condition and high strength after STA processing.
operating_temperature_band: High-strength beta titanium analogue rather than a hot-section high-temperature alloy; use where high strength and lower modulus are valued.
application_notes: High-strength springs, structural components and specialized aerospace hardware needing beta titanium behavior.
known_watch_outs: Do not use as a generic Ti64 replacement; beta alloy processing and spring-back behavior are different.
match_guardrails: Prefer for Ti candidates with strong beta stabilizer signatures (V/Cr/Mo/Zr), not for classic Ti64-style alpha-beta compositions.

nominal_composition_text: Ti balance, Al about 6, Sn about 2, Zr about 4, Mo about 2, Si minor
product_forms: bar; billet; forgings; compressor hardware
heat_treatment_summary: Stress-relief annealing from about 900-1300F is noted by ATI; use intermediate-temperature alpha-beta titanium heat-treatment logic.
joining_fabrication_notes: Intermediate-temperature Ti alloy analogue suited to forged compressor hardware rather than the hottest Ni/Co hot-section parts.
operating_temperature_band: ATI positions 6-2-4-2 as an intermediate-temperature titanium alloy with density about 4.54 g/cc and beta transus near 1825F.
application_notes: Compressor discs, rings and other elevated-temperature lightweight aerospace hardware.
known_watch_outs: Not a Ni/Co hot-section analogue; treat as a higher-temperature Ti route with oxidation and process control discipline.
match_guardrails: Use when lightweight intermediate-temperature performance matters; do not use for 850-1000C Ni/Co creep-led prompts.

product_forms: bar; billet; compressor discs; impellers
heat_treatment_summary: Near-beta titanium alloy; use solution/age or forged alpha-beta route logic rather than Ti64-only assumptions.
joining_fabrication_notes: Better treated as a high-strength intermediate-temperature forged titanium analogue for compressor and disc hardware.
operating_temperature_band: Official sources position Ti6246 as a high-strength intermediate-temperature near-beta titanium alloy for compressor discs and impellers.
application_notes: Impellers, compressor discs and other high-strength aerospace rotating hardware.
known_watch_outs: Higher density and more demanding processing than Ti64; still not a true Ni/Co hot-section analogue.
match_guardrails: Use when Ti family and high-strength intermediate-temperature service fit; do not force-match to Ti64 if Mo-rich near-beta behavior matters.
provenance_note: ATI product page + Aubert & Duval datasheet

spec_ids: AMS 4930; AMS 4931; AMS 4996; ASTM F136; ASTM F3001; ASTM F3302
nominal_composition_text: Ti-6Al-4V ELI class; same Ti/Al/V family as Ti64 with lower oxygen, carbon and iron than standard Grade 5
composition_vector_json: {"Ti": "balance", "Al": "Ti-6Al-4V class", "V": "Ti-6Al-4V class", "interstitials": "ELI / lower O,C,Fe than Grade 5"}
product_forms: bar; wire; billet; rings; powder
heat_treatment_summary: Normally used annealed in ATI sheet: anneal 1300-1350F for 1-8 h, air cool; stress relieve 900-1200F for 1-4 h. EOS Grade 23 positioning supports HIP-centric AM users.
joining_fabrication_notes: Higher-purity Ti64 variant with better formability and weld toughness than standard Grade 5; inert-gas protection is needed in fusion welding.
operating_temperature_band: Preferred for some cryogenic and aerospace uses; EOS Grade 23 positioning supports additive workflows where HIP is a standard post-treatment.
application_notes: Cryogenic-capable aerospace components; high-toughness Ti64 ELI use cases; medical and marine/cryogenic overlap exists in source literature.
known_watch_outs: Keep recipe claims cautious unless route/source is explicitly Grade 23-backed; preserve low-interstitial processing discipline.
match_guardrails: Do not force-match to standard Grade 5 if toughness / low-interstitial behavior is the real reason the candidate is attractive; keep ELI as a distinct analogue.
provenance_note: ATI Grade 23 sheet + EOS Grade 23 AM sheet

spec_ids: UNS R56400; AMS 4928; AMS 4965; AMS 4967; ASTM B348; ASTM F1472; ASTM F2924; ASTM F3302
nominal_composition_text: Ti balance, Al 5.5-6.75, V 3.5-4.5 (Grade 5 Ti6Al4V chemistry class)
product_forms: bars; forgings; forging stock; powder
heat_treatment_summary: Anneal 1275-1400F for 0.5-2 h; stress relieve 1000-1200F for 1-8 h; solution treat 1675-1750F for 1 h, water quench; age 975-1025F for 4-8 h. EOS recommends heat treatment to reduce internal stresses and increase ductility.
joining_fabrication_notes: Most widely used titanium grade; AM parts can be machined, shot-peened and polished; forging and wrought routes are standard.
operating_temperature_band: Used from cryogenic temperatures to about 800F / 427C in the ATI sheet; lightweight aerospace structural alloy.
application_notes: Compressor blades, discs and rings; airframe components; pressure vessels; rocket engine cases; rotor hubs; fasteners.
known_watch_outs: AM parts are anisotropic and generally need stress-relief / post-treatment; control oxygen/nitrogen/hydrogen pickup during processing.
match_guardrails: Do not use for Ni/Co hot-section analogues just because high strength is needed; this is a lightweight Ti structural analogue.
provenance_note: ATI wrought data sheet + EOS AM data sheet

nominal_composition_text: Co balance, Ni 22, Cr 22, W 14, Fe max 3, La 0.03
product_forms: sheet; plate; fabricated hot-section components
heat_treatment_summary: Usually furnished solution heat treated; normally 2125-2175F and rapidly cooled or water quenched for optimal properties.
joining_fabrication_notes: Readily fabricated and formed by conventional techniques; good forming and welding characteristics are highlighted by Haynes.
operating_temperature_band: Very good oxidation resistance for prolonged exposures up to 2000F / 1095C is highlighted in the brochure.
application_notes: Combustion cans; transition ducts; afterburner components; other aerospace gas-turbine hot-section fabrications.
known_watch_outs: High Co/W burden will likely worsen sustainability and supply-risk scores; use conventional-fabrication route logic unless trusted AM evidence is added later.
match_guardrails: Do not use as a Ni-superalloy analogue just because Ni is present; keep Co-dominant chemistry as the decisive family cue.

nominal_composition_text: Co balance, Ni about 20, Cr about 20, W about 15
product_forms: sheet; plate; bar; wire; fabricated hot-section parts
heat_treatment_summary: Solid-solution-strengthened cobalt-base alloy; use conventional fabricated/wrought solution-annealed route logic.
joining_fabrication_notes: Haynes highlights conventional fabrication and forming, with good oxidation and sulfidation resistance for prolonged exposures.
operating_temperature_band: Very good oxidation resistance for prolonged exposures up to about 1800F / 980C is highlighted by Haynes.
application_notes: Established aerospace gas-turbine parts, bearings and high-temperature static applications.
known_watch_outs: Older cobalt analogue than 188; high Co/W content penalizes sustainability and supply risk and AM support is not established here.
match_guardrails: Keep Co dominance as the family cue; do not let Ni presence recategorize this analogue as nickel-based.

nominal_composition_text: Nb balance, Zr about 1
product_forms: sheet; plate; rod; wire; thermal shields
heat_treatment_summary: Use refractory niobium alloy route logic rather than Ni/Co/Ti heat-treatment templates; focus on wrought fabrication and protective-environment handling.
joining_fabrication_notes: ATI notes excellent fabricability and ductility, with availability in desired mill product forms.
operating_temperature_band: ATI positions Nb1Zr as a lower-cost refractory option for high-temperature, lower-load applications, with service framing around 1800-2200F.
application_notes: Load-free thermal shields and other lower-load high-temperature refractory applications.
known_watch_outs: Less strong than C-103 or FS-85; oxidation/protective-environment treatment remains important.
match_guardrails: Use when a low-alloy niobium refractory analogue is appropriate; do not force-match to Hf/Ta/W-rich niobium systems.

spec_ids: ASTM B652; ASTM B654; ASTM B655
nominal_composition_text: Nb-10Hf-1Ti refractory alloy (niobium base with hafnium and titanium)
product_forms: sheet; rod; wire; powder/AM study context
heat_treatment_summary: Starter row does not fix a single certified heat-treatment window; Elmet AM paper discusses LPBF parts followed by HIP, coating and further heat treatment to simulate service conditions.
joining_fabrication_notes: Materion highlights good weldability and product availability in sheet/rod/wire; Elmet shows LPBF feasibility for C103 in an aerospace context.
operating_temperature_band: Elmet notes historic space-propulsion use around operating temperatures near 1300C for thruster-nozzle environments.
application_notes: Rocket nozzles; satellites; launch vehicles; jet-engine afterburner flap sections; high-temperature aerospace components.
known_watch_outs: High-temperature oxidizing service typically needs a protective coating such as R512E; HIP/coating can reduce AM strength versus wrought specifications.
match_guardrails: Do not treat as a generic Mo- or W-based refractory analogue; keep Nb-Hf-Ti chemistry and coating need explicit in recipe generation.
provenance_note: Materion product page + Elmet AM paper

nominal_composition_text: Nb balance, Ta 28, W 10, Zr 1
product_forms: powder; sheet; rod; propulsion hardware
heat_treatment_summary: Treat as a specialist niobium refractory route with LPBF/HIP/coating options rather than a generic wrought-only alloy.
joining_fabrication_notes: Elmet markets FS-85 powder for additive manufacturing and emphasizes high-temperature strength, corrosion resistance and weldability.
operating_temperature_band: Use as an extreme-temperature niobium refractory analogue for propulsion and specialist aerospace hardware rather than mainstream aircraft structure.
application_notes: Propulsion and high-temperature aerospace hardware requiring stronger niobium-alloy performance than Nb1Zr.
known_watch_outs: Oxidation/coating needs remain explicit; sustainability and cost burden are high because of Ta and W content.
match_guardrails: Do not use as a Mo-based refractory analogue; keep Nb-Ta-W-Zr chemistry and coating/AM specialist handling explicit.

nominal_composition_text: Mo base with about 0.5% Ti, 0.08% Zr and 0.01-0.04% C
product_forms: rods; plates/sheet; refractory components
heat_treatment_summary: Use specialist refractory processing logic rather than generic superalloy heat-treatment windows; treat as a powder-metallurgy / refractory-working analogue in v1.
joining_fabrication_notes: Plansee positions TZM as stronger and harder than pure molybdenum over a wide temperature range due to fine carbide additions.
operating_temperature_band: High-temperature refractory use; use as a refractory-family anchor rather than as a direct aircraft-certified alloy recipe source.
application_notes: Refractory high-temperature tooling and hot-zone style anchor; useful as a Mo-Ti-Zr refractory analogue.
known_watch_outs: Avoid over-claiming aerospace certification or AM readiness from this starter row; oxidation/protective-environment logic needs specialist treatment.
match_guardrails: Do not use when Ni/Co-based hot-section fabrication logic is required; this row is for refractory-family matching and specialist route generation.
